data.name: Dr. Danielle Jenkins
data.meta_title: Dr. Danielle Jenkins - Raleigh Hearing
data.meta_description: Meet Dr. Danielle Jenkins, Au.D., certified Lenire provider specializing in tinnitus and hearing care at Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, Raleigh, NC.

children.text: Danielle Jenkins is an audiologist providing hearing tests, hearing aids, and other hearing care services in Raleigh, NC.

Dr. Danielle Jenkins brings clinical expertise, compassion, and a strong commitment to patient-centered care to Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center. She believes that better hearing is about more than sound. It’s about staying connected to the people and moments that matter most.

Originally from Tampa, Florida, Dr. Jenkins earned her Doctorate in Audiology (Au.D.) from Pacific University in 2021. She completed her Bachelor of Science in Communication Sciences and Disorders at the University of Central Florida in 2016, where she was also a member of the NCAA collegiate volleyball team. Her academic and athletic background reflects the discipline, teamwork, and focus she brings to her clinical work every day.
Credentials and Professional Affiliations

Dr. Jenkins is licensed by the North Carolina Board of Examiners for Speech Language Pathologists and Audiologists and holds her Certificate of Clinical Competence in Audiology (CCC-A) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).
She is an active member of several professional organizations, including:
American Academy of Audiology
North Carolina Academy of Audiology
Academy of Doctors of Audiology
American Tinnitus Association
These affiliations reflect her commitment to evidence-based care and ongoing professional growth.
Special Interest in Tinnitus Care

Dr. Jenkins has a strong desire to help individuals who feel disconnected from loved ones due to hearing challenges. During her final year as an extern at the UNC Greensboro Speech and Hearing Center, she developed a particular passion for supporting patients with tinnitus.

She specializes in tinnitus management and is a certified Lenire Provider, offering advanced, research-backed options for individuals seeking relief from tinnitus symptoms. Her approach is thoughtful, personalized, and focused on improving quality of life, not just managing symptoms.
A Personal, Supportive Approach

Patients often appreciate Dr. Jenkins’ calm, approachable style and her ability to explain complex information in clear, understandable terms. She takes time to listen, answer questions, and work closely with each patient to develop solutions that fit their needs, goals, and daily life.
When she’s not caring for patients, Dr. Jenkins enjoys exploring the outdoors, creating new recipes, and spending time with family and friends. She also loves relaxing at home with her cat, Gus.
Schedule an Appointment
Dr. Danielle Jenkins is proud to serve patients at Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, where compassionate care and clinical excellence come together.
If you’re looking for expert guidance in hearing health or tinnitus care, she is here to help you take the next step with confidence.

faq_items.question: How do I know if I need a hearing aid?
faq_items.answer: If conversations are becoming harder to follow (especially in groups or noisy environments) or if you’re frequently turning up the TV or asking others to repeat themselves, these may be signs of hearing changes. Some patients also notice increased listening fatigue or frustration during social situations. A comprehensive hearing evaluation at Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center can provide clear answers and help determine whether hearing aids or other solutions are appropriate for you.

faq_items.question: Are hearing aids covered by insurance?
faq_items.answer: Insurance coverage for hearing aids varies depending on your individual plan. Some private insurance policies offer hearing aid benefits, while traditional Medicare typically does not.

faq_items.question: How often should I get a hearing test?
faq_items.answer: For most adults, hearing should be evaluated every one to two years, depending on age, medical history, and any changes you notice. If you are over 50, have a history of noise exposure, experience tinnitus, or already wear hearing aids, annual testing is often recommended. Regular hearing evaluations allow us to monitor changes early and help you maintain your best possible hearing health.

faq_items.question: How can hearing aids help with tinnitus?
faq_items.answer: Many patients experience tinnitus relief with hearing aids. By restoring access to everyday sounds, hearing aids can reduce the contrast between tinnitus and silence, making the ringing or buzzing less noticeable. Some devices also include built-in tinnitus therapy features for added support. Our audiologist will work closely with you to recommend solutions that address both your hearing needs and the impact tinnitus has on your daily life.

children.text: Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center is an audiology and hearing aid clinic with expert audiologists providing hearing tests and professional hearing services in Raleigh, NC.
Today’s hearing technology is more powerful, precise, and user-friendly than ever before, but technology alone is not the solution. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we believe
better hearing starts with understanding you
What makes the difference is expert care, thoughtful testing, and a team that takes the time to listen.
Our Doctors of Audiology combine advanced hearing technology with personalized care to help patients hear more clearly, communicate more easily, and feel supported at every step of their journey.
Care That Begins With Listening
No two people hear the same way, and no two hearing solutions should be the same. That’s why our process begins with a comprehensive hearing evaluation. This detailed testing helps us understand:
The degree of hearing loss
How well speech is understood in real-life situations
Whether one or both ears are affected
How hearing challenges impact daily life

After testing, we meet with you (and your family or loved ones, if you wish) to clearly explain the results and discuss solutions that fit your lifestyle, goals, and comfort level. Our focus is always on helping you make informed decisions with confidence.
Advanced Technology, Personalized Solutions
We offer state-of-the-art hearing aids that are designed to deliver:
Superior comfort
Clear, natural sound
Advanced features that support everyday listening
Easy-to-use controls and connectivity
Our expertise allows us to match the right technology to your specific needs, then carefully program and fine-tune it so it works in the environments that matter most to you.
Exceptional Care Beyond the Appointment

Hearing care does not end once hearing aids are selected. Ongoing support is a key part of long-term success. Our team works closely together to provide follow-up care, adjustments, education, and guidance as your hearing needs change over time.
We are proud to serve patients of all ages and to offer care that is professional, personal, and focused on real-world results.
Community Involvement and Hearing Screenings

Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center is active in the local community. We participate in wellness expos and offer hearing screenings throughout the area. If you are part of a business, organization, or group interested in on-site hearing screenings, we welcome you to contact us for more information.
Our Core Values
Everything we do is guided by a clear set of values:
Care and compassion for every life we touch
Respect and appreciation for each other
Teamwork and enjoyment in working together
Focus and discipline in improving the quality of care
Creativity and innovation to develop effective solutions
Honesty and integrity in all dealings
Our Mission

The licensed Doctors of Audiology at Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center strive to provide comprehensive, professional hearing healthcare for individuals of all ages. Our care is tailored to each person and reflects the attention, respect, and clinical expertise needed to support success. Our mission is to help every patient move toward a better quality of life through improved hearing.
Start Your Hearing Journey With Confidence
Whether you are noticing hearing changes for the first time, managing tinnitus, or looking for long-term hearing support, our team is here to help.
Contact Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center today to schedule an appointment.

children.text: Assistive listening devices (ALDs) are tools that help you hear more clearly in specific situations like watching TV, talking on the phone, or sitting in meetings.
They work alongside hearing aids or on their own to improve speech clarity, reduce background noise, and make listening less tiring.
Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center provides personalized assistive listening devices in Raleigh, NC to support real-life communication needs.

Assistive listening devices can make a big difference when hearing aids alone are not enough. Even with the best hearing technology, some places are still hard. Background noise, distance, or poor room acoustics can make speech unclear. Assistive listening devices give you extra support so you can stay connected and confident in everyday life.

At Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center, we take time to understand where hearing feels hardest for you. Then we recommend assistive listening devices that fit your needs, your lifestyle, and your comfort.
What Are Assistive Listening Devices?
Assistive listening devices are tools designed to make speech clearer in specific environments. They
do not
replace hearing aids. Instead, they add focused support when you need it most.
For example, you may hear fairly well in quiet places but struggle:
During family dinners
In church or large gatherings
While watching TV
On phone calls
In work meetings
Assistive listening devices help bring the speaker’s voice closer to you and reduce background noise. This can lower listening effort and help you feel less tired at the end of the day.
Types of Assistive Listening Devices We Offer
We provide several types of assistive listening devices based on your goals.
CaptionCall Phones

CaptionCall is a special telephone designed for individuals who have difficulty hearing on the phone. It works like a regular phone but also displays written captions of what the other person is saying on a large, easy-to-read screen.
This allows you to:
Read along while listening
Catch words you may have missed
Feel more confident during phone calls
Reduce frustration or misunderstandings
CaptionCall phones are often available at little to no cost for people with hearing loss who qualify. We can help you determine eligibility, complete paperwork, and guide you through setup.
For many patients, CaptionCall is one of the most helpful assistive listening devices for staying connected with family and friends.
Cell Phone Accessories

Cell phones are part of everyday life. But they can be hard to hear clearly, especially in noisy places or when sound quality is poor. Cell phone accessories can improve clarity and make calls more comfortable.
These options may include:
Direct Bluetooth streaming from your phone to your hearing aids
Remote microphones for clearer speech during calls
Hands-free calling support
App-based volume and sound adjustments
We help you pair your phone properly, adjust settings, and understand how everything works together. This makes using assistive listening devices with your cell phone simple and stress-free.
Personal Amplifiers
Personal amplifiers are small, portable devices that make nearby sounds louder. These may be helpful for short-term or situational use.
However, they are not medical hearing devices and do not replace a full hearing evaluation. We help you understand when this type of support is appropriate.
TV Streamers
TV streamers send sound directly from your television to your hearing aids or headphones. This allows you to:
Hear dialogue more clearly
Adjust the volume to your comfort
Watch TV without making it too loud for others
Many patients say this is one of the most helpful assistive listening devices for home use.
Remote Microphones
Remote microphones are small devices worn by a speaker or placed on a table. They pick up speech and send it directly to your hearing aids.
These are helpful for:
Meetings
Classrooms
Restaurants
Church services
Family gatherings
By bringing the speaker’s voice closer to you, remote microphones reduce the impact of distance and background noise.
Who Benefits from Assistive Listening Devices?
Assistive listening devices can help:
People who already wear hearing aids
People with mild hearing loss
Individuals who struggle most in noisy places
Anyone who feels tired from listening effort

Hearing loss is not the same for everyone. Some people need more help with distance. Others need support with noise. We guide you toward the right assistive listening devices based on your real-life challenges.
Why Professional Guidance Matters
It may be tempting to order devices online without guidance. But not all assistive listening devices work with every hearing aid. Some require proper pairing and setup.
At Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center, we:
Confirm compatibility with your hearing aids
Set up and pair devices for you
Teach you how to use them
Provide follow-up support
Troubleshoot any problems
You are not left to figure things out alone.
Hearing Care Is About Real Life
Our goal is not just to make sounds louder. It is to help you:
Follow conversations with confidence
Enjoy time with family
Participate in meetings
Watch TV comfortably
Feel connected again
Assistive listening devices are one more tool that supports that goal. When combined with hearing aids and personalized care, they can greatly improve daily communication.
Frequently Asked Questions
Do I need hearing aids to use assistive listening devices?
Not always. Some assistive listening devices can be used without hearing aids. However, many work best when paired with professionally fit hearing technology.
Are assistive listening devices hard to use?
Most devices are simple and user-friendly. We show you exactly how they work and answer all your questions during your visit.
Will these devices help in noisy places?
Yes. Remote microphones and similar tools are especially helpful in noisy environments because they focus on the speaker’s voice.
Can I use assistive listening devices at church or public events?
Many public spaces offer compatible systems. We can help you understand what options are available and how to connect.
Assistive Listening Devices in Raleigh, NC
If hearing still feels difficult in certain situations, you do not have to accept it. The right assistive listening devices can make everyday conversations clearer and less stressful.

At Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center, we provide personalized guidance and expert support for assistive listening devices in Raleigh, NC. We take time to listen, explain your options, and help you choose what truly fits your life.
Call Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center today to schedule an appointment and learn how assistive listening devices can help you hear with greater clarity and confidence.

children.text: Hearing Assessments

A hearing assessment provides a clear understanding of your hearing health and how it affects daily communication. We perform thorough diagnostic testing and take time to explain your results, so you leave with clarity and confidence about next steps.
Which hearing test is the best?
There isn’t a single “best” hearing test for everyone. The best hearing test is the one that matches your symptoms, concerns, and listening challenges.

For most patients, the most accurate and useful approach is a comprehensive audiological evaluation performed by an audiologist. This type of evaluation does not rely on just one test. Instead, it combines multiple tests to provide a complete picture of hearing health, speech understanding, and how hearing issues affect daily life.
How to know if I need a hearing test?
People often need a hearing test if conversations feel unclear, background noise is harder to manage, or listening feels tiring.
How to prepare for a hearing evaluation?
Preparing for a hearing evaluation involves noting symptoms, medical history, and any situations where hearing feels challenging.
Sensorineural Hearing Loss Evaluation & Treatment

Sensorineural hearing loss occurs when the inner ear or auditory nerve is affected and often develops gradually. We evaluate the cause and degree of hearing loss and recommend personalized treatment options designed to improve clarity and communication.
What are the most common signs of sensorineural hearing loss?

Sensorineural hearing loss generally makes speech sound muffled or unclear, especially in group settings or in noisy places. You might feel like people you are talking with are mumbling or notice that you’re turning up the TV more than usual. If conversations feel tiring or you’re missing important sounds, it may be time for a professional evaluation with an audiologist.
How is sensorineural hearing loss treated?
Treatment commonly includes hearing aids or other hearing technology selected based on hearing needs and lifestyle.
How to tell if sensorineural hearing loss is getting worse?
Worsening symptoms often include increased difficulty understanding speech, especially in noise, and higher listening fatigue.
Ear Wax Removal
Excess ear wax can block sound and cause discomfort, hearing changes, or tinnitus symptoms. Professional ear wax removal is a safe and gentle way to restore comfort and improve hearing clarity.
How do I know if ear wax is causing hearing problems?
Ear wax buildup often causes muffled hearing, a feeling of fullness in the ear, ringing sensations, or sudden changes in hearing clarity.
How is professional ear wax removal different from home methods?
Professional ear wax removal uses specialized tools and techniques that safely clear wax without pushing it deeper into the ear canal.
How often should ear wax be removed?
The frequency depends on how quickly wax builds up, but many patients benefit from periodic professional cleanings.
Tinnitus Evaluation

A tinnitus evaluation focuses on understanding how ringing or buzzing affects your hearing, focus, and quality of life. We identify contributing factors and use detailed testing to guide personalized management options.
Why should tinnitus be evaluated by an audiologist?
An audiologist evaluates hearing function, sound perception, and contributing factors that influence how tinnitus is experienced.
How do I know if my tinnitus needs professional care?
Tinnitus often benefits from professional care if it interferes with sleep, concentration, emotional well-being, or daily activities.
How long does a tinnitus evaluation take?
Most tinnitus evaluations take about one hour, depending on testing needs and discussion time.
Tinnitus Treatments

Tinnitus treatment focuses on reducing the daily impact of ringing or buzzing rather than eliminating sound entirely. Our treatment plans may include sound therapy, hearing technology, and education tailored to individual needs.
Can tinnitus be treated even if it cannot be cured?
Tinnitus can often be managed effectively using personalized strategies that reduce awareness and distress.
How effective are tinnitus treatments?
Many patients experience noticeable improvement in comfort, focus, and quality of life with consistent treatment.
How long does tinnitus treatment take to work?
Improvement typically occurs gradually over weeks or months as the brain adapts to management strategies.
Hearing Aid Services

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, our hearing aid services are designed to support you well beyond the initial fitting. We provide personalized hearing aid selection, precise fittings, ongoing adjustments, repairs, and routine maintenance to help your devices perform comfortably and reliably in your everyday life. Our team takes the time to understand your needs and provide continued support, so your hearing technology works for you today and for the long term.
Hearing Aid Fittings

A properly fitted hearing aid can make a meaningful difference in how comfortably and confidently you hear each day. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we take a personalized approach to hearing aid fittings, carefully programming and fine-tuning your devices using real-ear measurements and individualized adjustments. Our focus is on helping your hearing aids deliver clear, natural sound that fits your hearing needs and your lifestyle.
How long does it take to get fitted for a hearing aid?

A hearing aid fitting appointment typically takes about one to two hours. During this visit, we review your hearing evaluation, discuss the hearing aid options best suited to your needs, and program your devices to your personalized hearing profile. We also spend time showing you how to use, insert, remove, and care for your hearing aids comfortably. Follow-up visits are scheduled to fine-tune settings and support your adjustment over time.
How does an audiologist fit a hearing aid?

The fitting process begins with a detailed review of your hearing test results and listening goals. Based on this information, your audiologist selects and programs hearing aids using precise real-ear measurements to match amplification levels to your ears. The physical fit is adjusted for comfort, and you receive hands-on guidance for daily use, cleaning, and maintenance so you feel confident using your devices.
Can you get a hearing aid without a test?

Over-the-counter hearing aids are available without a hearing test, but they do not provide customized programming or professional support. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we recommend starting with a comprehensive hearing evaluation. This allows us to identify your specific hearing needs, recommend appropriate solutions, and rule out medical or structural concerns that could affect hearing outcomes.
Hearing Protection for Musicians
Musicians need protection that lowers volume without distorting sound quality. Custom musician hearing protection preserves clarity and balance while reducing the risk of noise-induced hearing loss.
Do musicians really need hearing protection?
Musicians are at higher risk for hearing loss due to prolonged exposure to amplified sound and repeated performances.
How can musicians protect their hearing without losing sound quality?
Custom musician earplugs maintain tonal balance while reducing harmful volume levels.
How long does musician hearing protection last?
With proper care, custom musician earplugs can last several years.
Hearing Aid Repair and Cleaning

Hearing aids are used every day, and regular care plays an important role in keeping them working properly. We provide professional hearing aid cleaning, troubleshooting, and repair services to help your devices stay clear, comfortable, and dependable. Whether you need routine maintenance or help with an unexpected issue, our team is here to support you and minimize interruptions to your hearing.
What are the common problems that require hearing aid repair?

Common hearing aid issues include weak or no sound, distorted or unclear audio, feedback or whistling, and physical wear or damage. Moisture and ear wax buildup can also affect performance. If you notice changes in sound quality or function, our team can evaluate your devices and recommend the appropriate solution.
How long does it take to repair a hearing aid?

Repair time depends on the issue. Many concerns, such as cleaning, replacing filters, or adjusting components, can often be handled in the office, sometimes the same day. More complex repairs that require manufacturer service may take several days to a couple of weeks. We explain the process and keep you informed throughout.
Can I fix my hearing aid at home?

Some basic troubleshooting, such as checking the battery, cleaning openings, or replacing a wax filter, can help resolve minor issues. However, more involved problems should be handled by a professional to avoid further damage. If at-home steps do not help, bringing your hearing aids to Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center allows us to safely assess and restore their performance.
Aural Rehabilitation

Aural rehabilitation focuses on improving listening skills, communication strategies, and confidence after hearing changes. It supports better outcomes with hearing aids and overall communication success.
Who benefits from aural rehabilitation?
Aural rehabilitation benefits individuals adjusting to hearing loss, hearing aids, or changes in listening environments.
How does aural rehabilitation improve daily communication?
It helps patients strengthen listening skills, manage challenging situations, and improve overall communication confidence.
How long does aural rehabilitation last?
The length varies, but many programs span several weeks or months based on individual goals.
Hearing Protection for Hunters
Hunting exposes ears to sudden impulse noise that can cause permanent damage. Specialized hearing protection reduces gunshot noise while allowing environmental awareness.
Why is hearing protection important for hunters?
Gunfire produces impulse noise levels that can cause permanent hearing damage from a single exposure.
How can hunters protect their hearing while staying aware?
Electronic or custom hearing protection reduces gunshot noise while allowing environmental sounds to remain audible.
How damaging is gunfire to hearing without protection?
A single gunshot can cause permanent hearing loss or tinnitus when hearing protection is not used.

children.text: Professional ear wax removal safely clears excess buildup that can cause muffled hearing, pressure, discomfort, or tinnitus symptoms.
Gentle, in-office removal can provide quick relief and help restore hearing clarity without the risks of at-home methods.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center offers safe, professional ear wax removal in Raleigh, NC.

Ear wax plays an important role in protecting your ears, but when it builds up or becomes trapped, it can interfere with hearing and comfort. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we provide professional ear wax removal in a calm, supportive environment to help relieve symptoms and restore clarity safely.

Our approach prioritizes comfort, education, and careful technique, so you feel at ease throughout your visit.
Understanding Ear Wax Buildup

Ear wax, also known as cerumen, is naturally produced to protect the ear canal by trapping dust and debris and providing lubrication. In most cases, wax moves out of the ear on its own. However, certain factors can cause wax to accumulate or become impacted, including:
Wearing hearing aids or earbuds
Narrow or curved ear canals
Dry skin or skin conditions
A history of ear infections
Naturally higher wax production
Using cotton swabs or at-home tools
When wax builds up faster than it clears, it can block the ear canal and affect hearing.
Signs of Excess Ear Wax
You may benefit from professional ear wax removal if you notice:
Muffled or reduced hearing
A feeling of fullness or pressure in the ear
Ringing or buzzing in the ears
Ear discomfort or itching
Dizziness or imbalance
Whistling or feedback from hearing aids
If these symptoms sound familiar, an evaluation can help determine whether wax buildup is the cause.
Why Professional Ear Wax Removal Matters

Many people attempt to remove ear wax at home using cotton swabs or other tools. While these methods seem convenient, they often push wax deeper into the ear canal and increase the risk of irritation or injury.
Professional ear wax removal offers a safer alternative by:
Removing wax under direct visualization
Reducing the risk of pushing wax deeper
Avoiding irritation or damage to the ear canal
Providing immediate symptom relief in many cases
Our Ear Wax Removal Process
At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, ear wax removal is performed by an experienced audiologist using gentle, proven techniques based on the type and location of the buildup.
Manual Removal
A small, specialized instrument is used to carefully lift and remove wax from the ear canal with precision and control.
Irrigation

For deeper or more stubborn buildup, gentle irrigation uses a controlled flow of warm water to safely flush wax from the ear canal. This method is comfortable, effective, and well-suited for patients with significant wax accumulation.
How Often Should Ear Wax Be Removed?
Most people do not need routine ear wax removal. However, more frequent care may be helpful if you:
Wear hearing aids
Experience recurring blockages
Have narrow ear canals
Produce excessive ear wax
Have skin conditions affecting the ears
Your audiologist will recommend a schedule based on your individual needs.
Ear Wax and Hearing Aids

Ear wax buildup is a common cause of hearing aid issues. Wax can block microphones or receivers, leading to weak sound, distortion, or feedback. Regular ear care can help hearing aids function more reliably and comfortably.
What to Avoid at Home

Safe at-home care includes wiping only the outer ear with a damp cloth or using drops to soften wax if recommended. Inserting objects into the ear canal or using ear candles increases the risk of injury and may worsen blockages.

If symptoms persist, professional care is the safest option.
Schedule Your Ear Wax Removal in Raleigh, NC

If you are experiencing blocked ears, discomfort, or changes in hearing, professional ear wax removal may provide relief.

Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center offers safe, gentle ear wax removal for patients throughout Raleigh, NC, with care focused on comfort and clarity.
Contact us
today to schedule your appointment!

children.text: A hearing aid fitting is a personalized process that programs and adjusts hearing aids to match your specific hearing needs and daily life.
Professional fitting and verification help hearing aids sound more natural, feel comfortable, and perform better in real-world listening environments.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides individualized hearing aid fittings for patients in Raleigh, NC.

Hearing aids are designed to do more than make sounds louder. They’re meant to help you hear more comfortably, clearly, and confidently. For hearing aids to truly work their best, they must be carefully programmed and adjusted by an audiologist who understands your unique hearing profile.
At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, hearing aid fittings are thoughtful, personalized, and focused on long-term success.
Why a Professional Hearing Aid Fitting Matters
A proper hearing aid fitting makes an immediate and lasting difference in how well you hear. When hearing aids are adjusted correctly:
Speech becomes easier to understand
Background noise feels less overwhelming
Sounds are more balanced and natural
Confidence in conversations improves
Your fitting is not just about how hearing aids sound in the office. It’s about how they support you in everyday situations, from quiet conversations to busy environments.
Why an Audiologist Should Fit Your Hearing Aids

Audiologists are trained to program hearing aids based on the exact pattern of your hearing loss, ear anatomy, and listening needs. Without professional fitting, hearing aids may feel uncomfortable or provide sound that is distorted or unclear.
At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, your audiologist focuses on making sure your hearing aids:
Fit securely and comfortably
Are programmed to your personal hearing profile
Provide accurate, meaningful sound enhancement
Support communication in real-life listening environments
Our goal is to help you wear your hearing aids comfortably from morning to night and feel supported every step of the way.
What to Expect During Your Hearing Aid Fitting
Your hearing aid fitting appointment is relaxed, informative, and tailored to you. During your visit, we guide you through each step of the process.
Review of Your Hearing Results
We begin by reviewing your hearing test and discussing the situations where hearing feels most challenging. This helps align your hearing aids with your communication goals.
Precise Programming
Your hearing aids are programmed using advanced software designed to match your specific hearing prescription.
Real Ear Measurement (REM)

Real ear measurement allows us to verify how your hearing aids perform inside your ear canal using real speech signals. Because every ear is different, this step supports accurate sound levels, clearer speech, and improved comfort.
Hands-On Instruction
Before you leave, we walk you through how to:
Insert and remove your hearing aids
Charge or change batteries
Clean and care for your devices
Use apps or wireless features, if applicable
Our goal is for you to feel confident using your new technology at home.
Scheduling Follow-Up Visits
Hearing aids often benefit from fine-tuning once you begin wearing them in everyday environments. Follow-up visits allow us to make adjustments and answer questions as you adapt.
What to Expect After Your First Fitting

Your first fitting marks the beginning of your hearing journey. As your brain adjusts to hearing new sounds again, everyday noises such as footsteps or your own voice may sound different at first. This adjustment period is normal and improves over time.
We remain available for guidance and support, helping your hearing aids continue to feel natural and perform reliably.
Adjusting to Hearing Aids
Getting used to hearing aids takes patience and consistency. Helpful tips during the adjustment period include:

Wearing your hearing aids consistently
Starting in quieter environments
Gradually introducing more challenging listening situations
Reaching out if something does not feel right
Most people adjust within a few weeks, and small changes can make a meaningful difference.
The Importance of Follow-Up Visits
Follow-up appointments allow us to adjust settings based on your real-world listening experiences. These visits support comfort, clarity, and long-term success with hearing aids.
Frequently Asked Questions
Are hearing aid fittings covered by insurance?
Insurance coverage varies by plan. Traditional Medicare typically does not cover hearing aids, while some private insurance or benefit programs may offer assistance.
How can I make my hearing aids more comfortable?
Comfort can often be improved through adjustments to fit, programming, or earmolds. Letting your audiologist know what you are experiencing allows for helpful changes.
How long does a hearing aid fitting take?
The initial hearing aid fitting usually takes about one hour, with additional follow-up visits scheduled as needed.
Schedule Your Hearing Aid Fitting

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we are committed to helping you hear clearly with hearing aids that fit well, feel comfortable, and support your daily life. If you’re ready to take the next step, we’re here to guide you.
Contact us
today to schedule your hearing aid fitting in Raleigh, NC. Begin your journey toward clearer, more confident hearing!

children.text: Sensorineural hearing loss evaluation identifies damage to the inner ear or auditory nerve that affects how clearly sound is processed.
Personalized treatment options can improve communication, reduce listening fatigue, and help you stay connected in daily life.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides comprehensive sensorineural hearing loss evaluation and treatment for patients in Raleigh, NC.

Sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) is one of the most common types of hearing loss and often develops gradually. It can make soft sounds harder to hear while causing louder sounds to feel unclear or distorted. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we provide thorough evaluation, clear diagnosis, and thoughtful treatment planning to help you better understand your hearing and move forward with confidence.Our care is centered on listening to your concerns, explaining findings clearly, and recommending solutions that fit your needs, lifestyle, and goals.
Understanding Sensorineural Hearing Loss

Sensorineural hearing loss occurs when the delicate structures of the inner ear or the auditory nerve are affected. Inside the inner ear are thousands of tiny hair cells that send sound information to the brain. When these cells are damaged, they do not regenerate, which is why SNHL is typically permanent.While this type of hearing loss cannot be reversed, modern audiology care offers effective ways to improve clarity, communication, and overall quality of life.
Why Evaluation and Treatment Matter
Untreated sensorineural hearing loss can affect more than just hearing. Over time, it may contribute to:
Increased listening fatigue
Difficulty following conversations
Frustration in social situations
Feelings of isolation or withdrawal
Ringing or buzzing in the ears (tinnitus)
With professional evaluation and personalized treatment, many patients experience meaningful improvements in how they hear, communicate, and engage with others.
Common Signs of Sensorineural Hearing Loss
Sensorineural hearing loss may develop slowly or appear more suddenly. Common signs include:
Difficulty hearing soft or high-pitched sounds
Feeling like others are mumbling
Frequently asking for repetition
Trouble following conversations, especially in noise
Ringing or buzzing in one or both ears
Hearing better in one ear than the other
If any of these concerns sound familiar, a comprehensive hearing evaluation can provide clear answers.
What Causes Sensorineural Hearing Loss
Sensorineural hearing loss can have many causes, including:
Age-related changes in hearing
Prolonged or repeated noise exposure
Genetic factors
Certain medications that affect the inner ear
Head or ear injuries
Understanding the underlying cause helps guide the most appropriate treatment approach.
How We Evaluate Sensorineural Hearing Loss

Evaluation begins with a comprehensive hearing test designed to measure both hearing sensitivity and speech understanding. We take time to review your hearing history, symptoms, and listening challenges, then explain results in a clear and supportive way.If sensorineural hearing loss is identified, we discuss what the findings mean for your daily life and outline appropriate treatment options based on your comfort level and goals.
Treatment Options for Sensorineural Hearing Loss
Although sensorineural hearing loss cannot be cured, today’s hearing technology offers effective ways to improve communication and listening comfort.
Hearing Aids

Hearing aids are commonly recommended for mild to moderate sensorineural hearing loss. Modern devices are discreet, comfortable, and designed to improve speech clarity while managing background noise.
Advanced Hearing Solutions

For more significant hearing loss, additional options may be discussed, including implantable hearing solutions or alternative technologies, depending on individual needs.We guide you through each option and help you make informed decisions without pressure.
Sensorineural Hearing Loss and Tinnitus

Tinnitus often occurs alongside sensorineural hearing loss, even when hearing changes are subtle. Treating hearing loss can help reduce the contrast between tinnitus and silence, making ringing or buzzing less noticeable for many patients.When appropriate, treatment plans are designed to support both hearing clarity and tinnitus comfort.
Protecting Your Hearing Going Forward
While existing sensorineural hearing loss cannot be reversed, steps can be taken to protect remaining hearing, including:
Using hearing protection in loud environments
Limiting prolonged noise exposure
Scheduling regular hearing evaluations
Addressing hearing changes early
Early detection and ongoing care play an important role in long-term hearing health.
Frequently Asked Questions
Is sensorineural hearing loss permanent?
Sensorineural hearing loss is typically permanent because damaged inner-ear hair cells do not regenerate. Treatment focuses on improving hearing through technology and personalized care.
Do hearing aids really help with sensorineural hearing loss?
Modern hearing aids are specifically designed to support damaged hearing pathways and often provide significant improvement in speech understanding and listening comfort.
Can sensorineural hearing loss affect one ear or both?
Sensorineural hearing loss may affect one ear or both. Age-related hearing loss commonly affects both ears, while other causes may impact one side more than the other.
Sensorineural Hearing Loss Care in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, our goal is to help you hear more clearly, communicate more comfortably, and feel supported throughout your care. We provide thorough evaluation, personalized treatment options, and long-term support for sensorineural hearing loss.
Contact us
today to schedule your hearing evaluation in Raleigh, NC!

children.text: Custom hearing protection reduces harmful noise while preserving awareness of important sounds in everyday and high-noise environments.
Individually molded protection offers superior comfort, secure fit, and consistent performance compared to one-size-fits-all options.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides personalized custom hearing protection in Raleigh, NC.

Once hearing is damaged, it cannot be restored naturally. That’s why protecting your hearing is just as important as treating hearing loss. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we help patients protect their hearing with thoughtfully selected hearing protection solutions designed for comfort, durability, and long-term use.

Whether you work in noisy environments, enjoy music, spend time hunting or shooting, swim regularly, or simply want a quieter night’s sleep, the right hearing protection can make a meaningful difference.
Why Hearing Protection Matters
Repeated exposure to loud sounds can cause permanent hearing damage over time. Even everyday activities, such as concerts, power tools, or recreational shooting, can place stress on the ears.
Hearing protection helps:
Reduce the risk of noise-related hearing loss
Support long-term hearing health
Improve comfort in loud environments
Allow safer participation in work and hobbies
Custom Hearing Protection Options
Custom hearing protection is created specifically for your ears, offering a secure and comfortable fit that works well for extended wear.
Custom Earplugs

Custom earplugs are designed for individuals regularly exposed to noise, including musicians, industrial workers, and hobbyists. They provide consistent noise reduction while remaining comfortable throughout the day.
Impulse Filter Hearing Protection
Impulse-filter protection is commonly used by hunters and shooters. These devices soften sudden loud sounds while allowing awareness of voices and surrounding movement.
Sleep Plugs
Custom sleep plugs are soft and flexible, designed to reduce nighttime noise without causing pressure or discomfort.
Musician In-Ear Monitors (IEMs)
Custom in-ear monitors provide clear, high-quality sound while helping musicians maintain safe listening levels during practice and performance.
Digital Hearing Protection
Advanced digital protection systems amplify softer sounds while instantly reducing harmful noise, making them well-suited for hunting and high-noise environments.
Hearing Protection for Every Lifestyle
Custom hearing protection can be designed for a wide range of needs, including:
Music and performing
Hunting and shooting
Swimming
Sleeping
Industrial and occupational settings
Outdoor and recreational activities
Materials Used for Custom Ear Molds
Custom ear molds are available in several materials to match comfort and use preferences:
Acrylic: Smooth, durable, and easy to clean
Vinyl: Softer and often preferred for sensitive ears
Silicone: Flexible and water-resistant, ideal for swimming and extended wear
How Custom Hearing Protection Is Made
The process of creating custom hearing protection is simple and comfortable:
An audiologist takes an impression of your ear using soft molding material
The impression is sent to a specialized laboratory
Your custom hearing protection is created based on your selected style and material
The result is a secure, personalized fit designed for reliable use.
How Long Does Custom Hearing Protection Last?

With proper care, custom hearing protection typically lasts three to five years. Fit and performance may change over time due to natural changes in the ears, which is why periodic checks are recommended.
Frequently Asked Questions
How do I clean custom earplugs or ear molds?
Custom earplugs can be cleaned with a soft cloth. Mild soap and warm water may be used if needed, as long as the plugs are fully dried before reuse.
Can custom hearing protection be worn with helmets or safety gear?
Custom hearing protection fits entirely in the ear canal, making it compatible with helmets, hard hats, and other safety equipment.
Do custom earplugs block all sound?
Custom earplugs reduce harmful noise levels while still allowing important sounds, such as speech or environmental cues, to be heard.
Custom Hearing Protection in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we are committed to helping patients protect their hearing for the long term. From everyday solutions to advanced custom hearing protection, our care is focused on comfort, safety, and individualized support.

today to learn more about custom hearing protection in Raleigh, NC.

children.text: Hearing aids and related products are designed to improve clarity, comfort, and communication in everyday listening environments.
Today’s hearing technology offers advanced features that support speech understanding, background noise management, and tinnitus comfort.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides hearing aids and hearing products in Raleigh, NC.

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we believe better hearing supports a more connected, confident life. That’s why we offer a carefully selected range of hearing aids and hearing-related products from trusted manufacturers, along with professional guidance and long-term support.
Whether you are exploring hearing aids for the first time or looking for ongoing care and solutions, our focus is on helping you find technology that fits your hearing needs, lifestyle, and goals.
Hearing Aids We Offer

Hearing aids are not one-size-fits-all. The best hearing aid is the one that supports your daily listening needs while feeling comfortable and natural. We work closely with you to understand your hearing challenges and recommend options that align with how and where you listen most.
We offer advanced hearing aids from leading manufacturers, including:
Phonak Hearing Aids
Oticon Hearing Aids
ReSound Hearing Aids
Widex Hearing Aids
Starkey Hearing Aids
Signia Hearing Aids

These brands offer a wide range of styles and technology levels, including discreet designs, rechargeable options, Bluetooth connectivity, background-noise support, and features that may help manage tinnitus symptoms.
Personalized Hearing Aid Selection
Choosing hearing aids involves more than comparing features. We help you understand how different technologies may support:
Conversations in quiet and noisy settings
Phone calls and media streaming
Comfort throughout the day
Long-term ease of use and maintenance
Our recommendations are based on your hearing evaluation, lifestyle, and personal preferences, so you can make informed decisions with confidence.
Hearing Aid Support, Repair, and Maintenance

Hearing aids require ongoing care to perform reliably. We provide professional support services, including cleanings, troubleshooting, adjustments, and repairs. Even if your hearing aids were purchased elsewhere, we are often able to help keep them functioning as intended.
Regular maintenance helps extend the life of your devices and supports consistent sound quality.
Custom Hearing Protection and Ear Products
In addition to hearing aids, we offer custom hearing products designed to protect and support your hearing health.
Custom Hearing Protection
Custom hearing protection helps reduce harmful noise exposure while allowing awareness of important sounds. These products are commonly used for work, hobbies, and recreational activities.
Custom Ear Molds

Custom ear molds are designed for comfort and a secure fit and may be used for hearing aids, in-ear monitors, headsets, or swim plugs. A custom fit can improve comfort and reduce feedback or sound leakage.
Assistive Listening and Hearing Accessories

Some listening environments benefit from additional support beyond hearing aids alone. We offer hearing-related accessories and assistive listening options designed to improve clarity and convenience in specific situations, such as phone calls or group settings.
Frequently Asked Questions
Which hearing aids are the most advanced?

Many modern hearing aids offer advanced features such as rechargeable batteries, Bluetooth connectivity, and improved background-noise management. The best option depends on your individual hearing needs and lifestyle.
Do hearing aids help with tinnitus?
Many hearing aids include features that can help reduce the impact of tinnitus by improving access to sound and, in some cases, offering tinnitus support programs.
How long do hearing aids typically last?
Most hearing aids last three to five years with proper care and regular maintenance.
Hearing Aids in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we are committed to helping you hear better with solutions that feel comfortable, practical, and supportive of your daily life. From advanced hearing aids to custom hearing products, our care is guided by experience, compassion, and attention to detail.
Contact us
today to schedule an appointment and learn more about hearing aids and products available in Raleigh, NC.

data.meta_description: Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides hearing aid batteries in Raleigh, NC, to make sure that your hearing devices always function at their best.

children.text: Hearing aid batteries power the features that help you hear clearly, stay connected, and manage everyday listening situations.
Disposable and rechargeable battery options offer flexibility based on hearing aid style, daily routine, and personal preference.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center provides hearing aid batteries, guidance, and support for patients in Raleigh, NC.

Hearing aid batteries play an essential role in keeping your hearing aids working reliably day after day. From amplifying sound to supporting Bluetooth streaming and tinnitus features, dependable battery power allows your devices to perform as intended.At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we help patients choose, maintain, and troubleshoot hearing aid batteries so their hearing technology remains consistent and dependable.
Types of Hearing Aid Batteries
Today’s hearing aids use either disposable or rechargeable battery systems. Each option offers benefits depending on your hearing aid model and lifestyle.
Disposable Zinc-Air Batteries
Disposable zinc-air batteries have been used in hearing aids for many years and provide steady, reliable power. They are available in multiple sizes, each identified by a standard color code:
Size 10 – Yellow
Size 312 – Brown
Size 13 – Orange
Size 675 – Blue
These batteries activate when the small tab is removed. Allowing the battery to sit for a few minutes after removing the tab helps it reach full power before use.
Rechargeable Lithium-Ion Batteries

Many modern hearing aids now include built-in rechargeable lithium-ion batteries. These systems provide all-day power with overnight charging, eliminating the need to handle small disposable batteries.Rechargeable batteries are often preferred by patients who want a simpler routine, have dexterity concerns, or prefer a more environmentally friendly option.
Silver-Zinc Rechargeable Batteries
Some hearing aids use removable silver-zinc rechargeable batteries. While less common today, we continue to support patients who use this battery type and provide guidance on proper use and care.
Choosing the Right Battery Option
The best hearing aid battery depends on several factors, including:
Your hearing aid model
How long you wear your hearing aids each day
Comfort with handling small batteries
Preference for charging versus replacing batteries
Our audiologist can help you understand your options and select the battery solution that best fits your daily routine.
Tips for Longer Battery Life
For Disposable Batteries
Wait a few minutes after removing the tab before inserting
Store batteries in a cool, dry place
Open the battery door at night to reduce moisture
Keep spare batteries in their original packaging
For Rechargeable Batteries
Charge hearing aids overnight so they are ready each morning
Use the charger designed for your specific hearing aids
Keep devices and chargers away from moisture and extreme heat
Turn hearing aids off when not in use
Signs a Battery Needs to Be Changed or Recharged
Hearing aids may alert you when power is low through tones, spoken messages, or indicator lights. Other signs include:
Fading or weak sound
Distorted audio
Sound cutting in and out
Devices shutting off unexpectedly
Replacing or recharging the battery often resolves these issues.
Troubleshooting Battery Concerns
If your hearing aids are not working properly, it may help to check:
Whether the correct battery size is being used
That the battery is inserted properly
That contacts are clean and free of debris
Whether the battery is fresh or fully charged
If concerns continue, bringing your hearing aids to the office allows us to evaluate and address the issue.
Frequently Asked Questions
How long do hearing aid batteries last?

Disposable hearing aid batteries typically last three to ten days depending on size and usage. Rechargeable batteries usually provide a full day of use and are designed to last several years before replacement.
Are rechargeable hearing aid batteries better for the environment?
Rechargeable batteries reduce waste by eliminating the need to dispose of multiple batteries each month, making them a more environmentally friendly option.
Do cold temperatures affect hearing aid batteries?
Cold temperatures can shorten battery life, so keeping hearing aids and spare batteries at room temperature is helpful.
Hearing Aid Batteries in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we offer a full range of hearing aid batteries and support services to help your devices perform consistently. Whether you need replacement batteries, guidance on rechargeable options, or help troubleshooting power issues, our team is here to make hearing care easier.
Contact us
today to learn more about hearing aid batteries and support in Raleigh, NC.

children.text: Lenire tinnitus management uses FDA-approved bimodal neuromodulation to retrain the brain’s response to ringing, buzzing, or phantom sounds.
Lenire is non-invasive, personalized, and completed at home with guidance from a Doctor of Audiology.
Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center provides comprehensive tinnitus evaluations and customized Lenire care in Raleigh, NC.

Lenire is an FDA-approved tinnitus management that uses advanced bimodal neuromodulation technology. By gently stimulating specific neural pathways connected to tinnitus, Lenire works to reduce the perception of ringing, buzzing, or phantom sounds.This non-invasive approach offers new hope for individuals struggling with chronic tinnitus, helping retrain the brain’s response to sound and improve daily comfort and quality of life.Tinnitus affects millions of people. For some, it is mild. For others, it can be constant and deeply disruptive. If tinnitus is interfering with your sleep, focus, or peace of mind, Lenire may be an option worth exploring.
What Is Tinnitus?
Tinnitus is the perception of sound without an external source. People often describe it as:
Ringing
Buzzing
Humming
Hissing
Clicking
The sound may be steady or come and go. It may affect one ear or both. It can range from soft and barely noticeable to loud and intrusive.Common symptoms of tinnitus include:
Persistent ringing or buzzing in the ears
Trouble concentrating
Sleep disturbances
Emotional stress or anxiety
Sensitivity to sound
Frustration in quiet environments

Tinnitus is not a disease itself. It is a symptom. It may be related to hearing loss, noise exposure, ear conditions, certain medications, or other medical factors. That is why a full evaluation is always the first step.
A New Approach: Lenire Tinnitus Management

Many traditional tinnitus treatments focus on masking the sound or helping you cope with it. While helpful for some, these approaches do not directly target the neural patterns involved in tinnitus.Lenire takes a different path.Lenire uses bimodal neuromodulation. This means it stimulates two sensory systems at the same time:
Sound stimulation through Bluetooth headphones
Gentle electrical stimulation delivered to the tongue
Together, these signals help retrain how the brain processes tinnitus.Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center provides Lenire tinnitus management in Raleigh, NC. Contact our office to learn more.
How Lenire Works
Lenire is built around the science of neuroplasticity, the brain’s ability to reorganize and form new connections.The system includes three parts:
Headphones
Customized sounds are delivered through Bluetooth headphones. These sounds activate the auditory pathways in the brain.
Tonguetip
A small, comfortable mouthpiece rests on the tongue. It delivers mild electrical pulses to stimulate nerves connected to the brain’s somatosensory system.
Controller

A handheld device allows you to start, pause, adjust volume, and control stimulation levels. Settings are customized by your audiologist based on your tinnitus profile.By stimulating both the auditory system and the tongue at the same time, Lenire encourages the brain to reduce its focus on tinnitus. Over time, this can decrease the perceived intensity and emotional impact of the sound.
Clinical Research and Effectiveness
Lenire is the first bimodal neuromodulation device approved by the FDA for tinnitus management.Large-scale clinical trials have shown promising results. In one major study:
A majority of participants reported meaningful reductions in tinnitus severity
Many experienced improved sleep
Emotional distress related to tinnitus decreased
Benefits were sustained months after treatment
While results vary from person to person, research supports Lenire is a safe and effective option for many individuals with chronic tinnitus.
Preparing for Lenire
Step 1: Comprehensive Evaluation
Your first appointment includes:
Full diagnostic hearing testing
Tinnitus assessment
Medical history review
Discussion of symptom severity and daily impact
Not every patient is a candidate for Lenire. A thorough evaluation helps determine whether this treatment aligns with your specific needs.
Step 2: Lenire Fitting Appointment
If Lenire is recommended, a second visit is scheduled for device fitting and programming.During this appointment:
Device settings are customized
You are trained on how to use the system
Treatment expectations are reviewed
A structured plan is created
Treatment sessions are then completed at home, with follow-up visits to monitor progress and make adjustments if needed.
Frequently Asked Questions
Is Lenire safe?

Lenire is non-invasive and has been shown to be well-tolerated in clinical studies. Most patients report minimal side effects. An audiologist will complete a comprehensive evaluation to determine if Lenire is appropriate for you before beginning care.
How long does Lenire treatment take?

Management plans typically last several weeks to a few months. Most patients use the device daily at home for prescribed sessions. Follow-up visits allow your audiologist to monitor progress and make adjustments as needed.
How soon will I notice results with Lenire tinnitus treatment?

Some patients notice improvement within a few weeks, while others require longer, consistent use. Clinical studies suggest meaningful changes often occur over several weeks to months. Results vary depending on the individual and the severity of tinnitus.
Does Lenire cure tinnitus?

Lenire is not considered a cure. Instead, it works to reduce the perception and emotional impact of tinnitus. Many patients report that the sound becomes less noticeable and less disruptive to daily life.
Can Lenire be used with other tinnitus treatments?

Yes. Lenire can often be combined with other tinnitus management strategies such as hearing aids, sound therapy, counseling, or aural rehabilitation. Your audiologist will help determine the safest and most effective plan for your needs.
Do I need hearing loss to qualify for Lenire?
No. Lenire can be appropriate for individuals with or without hearing loss. A hearing evaluation is part of the assessment process to understand your complete auditory health.
Lenire Tinnitus Management in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing & Tinnitus Center, tinnitus care is evidence-based and personalized. We understand how disruptive persistent ringing can be. Our goal is to help you regain a sense of calm and control.
If you are ready to explore Lenire tinnitus management in Raleigh, NC, we invite you to schedule a consultation.
Our experienced audiologists can provide clear answers, compassionate care, and expert guidance every step of the way.

tags

SHARING YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION
We may share your Personal Information with third parties as needed to help us use your Personal Information, as described above.

We also use Google Analytics to help us understand how our customers use the Site–you can read more about how Google uses your Personal Information here: https://www.google.com/intl/en/policies/privacy/. You can also opt out of Google Analytics here: https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Finally, we may also share your Personal Information to comply with applicable laws and regulations, to respond to a subpoena, search warrant, or other lawful request for information we receive, or to otherwise protect our rights.

For more information about how targeted advertising works, you can visit the Network Advertising Initiative’s (“NAI”) educational page at http://www.networkadvertising.org/understanding-online-advertising/how-does-it-work.

data.meta_description: Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center is an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Raleigh, NC. Contact us to learn more!

children.text: Oticon hearing aids are built on research focused on how the brain processes sound, supporting more natural and balanced listening.
Advanced Oticon technology helps reduce listening effort, improve speech clarity, and support comfort in noisy or dynamic environments.
Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center is an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Raleigh, NC.

Oticon is one of the most trusted names in hearing technology, known for decades of research centered on how the brain understands sound. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we recommend Oticon hearing aids for patients who want hearing that feels natural, comfortable, and supportive of everyday communication.
Each Oticon device is carefully selected, professionally fit, and verified to align with your hearing needs, lifestyle, and long-term goals.
Why Choose Oticon Hearing Aids

Oticon’s approach goes beyond simply making sounds louder. Their BrainHearing™ philosophy is designed to give your brain better access to sound, helping conversations feel clearer while reducing listening effort throughout the day.
Oticon hearing aids are often a good fit for individuals who:
Feel mentally fatigued after listening
Struggle to follow conversations in background noise
Want hearing that sounds more natural and balanced
Value research-driven, evidence-based technology
Oticon Hearing Aid Models We Offer
We offer and support a wide range of Oticon hearing aid models, allowing us to match technology to your hearing needs and preferences.
Oticon Zeal

Oticon Zeal is designed for individuals who want advanced hearing technology in an ultra-discreet, invisible style. Built on Oticon’s BrainHearing philosophy, Zeal supports natural sound processing while remaining completely unseen in the ear.
Key features include:
Invisible-in-the-ear design options
BrainHearing technology for reduced listening effort
Wireless connectivity for compatible devices
Rechargeable options available
Oticon Intent

Oticon Intent uses advanced sensor technology to adapt to your listening environment in real time. By responding to movement, conversation cues, and surroundings, Intent supports clearer speech with less effort.
Key features include:
Real-time sound adaptation
Bluetooth LE Audio for high-quality streaming
Fast charging with dependable all-day power
Oticon Jet PX

Oticon Jet PX brings Oticon’s modern sound processing and Deep Neural Network technology to a flexible and accessible hearing solution. It is designed to improve speech clarity while managing background noise in everyday listening environments.
Key features include:
Deep Neural Network sound processing
Speech Guard technology for clearer speech
Wind Noise Management
Bluetooth connectivity
Tinnitus SoundSupport available
Oticon Real
Oticon Real is designed to reduce disruptive sounds such as wind or handling noise while preserving speech clarity, helping everyday listening feel more comfortable.
Key features include:
Environmental noise and wind reduction
Bluetooth streaming for compatible devices
Tinnitus SoundSupport options available
Oticon Own and Oticon Own SI
Oticon Own and Own SI are custom, in-the-ear hearing aids created for individuals who prefer a discreet solution. These devices are custom molded for comfort while delivering clear, natural sound.
Key features include:
Invisible-in-Canal (IIC) and Completely-in-Canal (CIC) options
BrainHearing technology in a custom-fit design
Durable, moisture-resistant construction
Oticon Zircon
Oticon Zircon provides 360-degree sound awareness, helping users stay engaged with their surroundings while reducing listening effort.
Key features include:
Direct Bluetooth streaming
App-based control and adjustments
Tinnitus SoundSupport available
Oticon Xceed
Oticon Xceed is designed for individuals with severe to profound hearing loss, providing powerful amplification while maintaining clarity and comfort.
Key features include:
Strong, stable amplification
Bluetooth streaming and app control
Tinnitus SoundSupport available
Oticon CROS
Oticon CROS is designed for individuals with single-sided hearing loss. It wirelessly transmits sound from the non-hearing ear to the better-hearing ear to improve awareness and balance.
Key features include:
Wireless sound transmission
App-based control
Compatibility with multiple Oticon platforms
Oticon Accessories and Assistive Technology

Oticon offers a range of accessories that support hearing in challenging situations, including phone calls, television listening, and group conversations. Options may include remote microphones, TV adapters, and wireless connectivity tools designed to improve clarity and convenience.
Ongoing Support for Oticon Hearing Aids

Our care does not end after fitting. We provide continued adjustments, cleanings, maintenance, and repair services for both current and older Oticon models. Even if your Oticon hearing aids were purchased elsewhere, we are happy to evaluate and support them.
Please contact us to learn more about Oticon hearing aids we repair and service.
Frequently Asked Questions
How long do Oticon hearing aids last?
With proper care and regular follow-up, Oticon hearing aids typically last three to five years.
Do Oticon hearing aids help with tinnitus?
Many Oticon models include Tinnitus SoundSupport, which may help reduce tinnitus awareness and improve listening comfort.
Which Oticon model is right for me?
The best Oticon model depends on your hearing needs, listening environments, and personal preferences. Your audiologist will guide you through options based on what matters most to you.
Oticon Hearing Aids in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we combine Oticon’s advanced technology with evidence-based fitting, real ear verification, and thoughtful follow-up care. Our goal is to help you hear with clarity, comfort, and confidence.
Contact us
today to schedule a consultation and explore Oticon hearing aids in Raleigh, NC.

Are Phonak hearing aids good for active lifestyles?

Phonak technology is designed to automatically manage background noise and support speech clarity in a variety of listening situations.
Phonak Hearing Aids in Raleigh, NC

At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we combine Phonak’s advanced hearing technology with evidence-based fitting, real ear verification, and patient-centered care. Our goal is to help you hear clearly, comfortably, and confidently in the moments that matter most.

All-day power, including during streaming
Protective coating for water and dust resistance
Bluetooth and Auracast support
App-based tools for quick access to public broadcasts

Key features include:
Reliable all-day battery performance
Weather-resistant coating
Bluetooth and Auracast support
Hands-free calling compatibility with select devices
ReSound Omnia
ReSound Omnia is designed to balance natural sound, comfort, and everyday connectivity. It is available in multiple styles to match a wide range of hearing needs and preferences.
ReSound Omnia RIE and miniRIE
Receiver-in-the-ear options for mild to severe hearing loss, available with rechargeable or disposable battery choices.
ReSound Omnia Custom ITE (ITC, CIC, IIC)
Custom in-the-ear options designed for a personalized fit with reduced interference from glasses and natural wind shielding.
ReSound Omnia BTE and Power BTE

Behind-the-ear and power options for broader ranges of hearing loss, including more significant needs. Omnia is recognized for supporting speech understanding in noise through its beamforming approach.

Natural sound and comfortable fit
Strong streaming and connectivity features
App-based support and remote care tools
ReSound Relief tinnitus support features
ReSound Apps and Support
ReSound offers app-based features that support comfort, confidence, and ongoing personalization, including:
Quick adjustments and custom listening programs
Remote fine-tuning options, when available
We help patients understand which tools are most helpful for their daily life and how to use them comfortably without feeling overwhelmed.

ReSound accessories can provide added clarity in situations where hearing is more challenging, such as distance listening, group conversations, or television viewing.
Common options include:
TV Streamer
Phone Clip
Multi Mic
Remote Control
Accessories are recommended based on your listening goals and how you prefer to interact with technology.
Ongoing Service and Support

Hearing aid care continues long after you choose a device. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we provide cleanings, maintenance, fine-tuning, manufacturer repair coordination, and follow-up visits based on your real-world listening experiences.
If you already own ReSound hearing aids, even if they were purchased elsewhere, we are happy to evaluate and support them. We may also service select older ReSound models.

How long do ReSound hearing aids last?
With regular care and maintenance, ReSound hearing aids typically last three to five years, depending on usage and environment.
Can ReSound hearing aids connect to my phone?
Many ReSound models offer direct smartphone connectivity for calls and streaming. Compatibility depends on your phone and the specific ReSound model.
Do ReSound hearing aids help with tinnitus?
Many ReSound hearing aids support tinnitus comfort through sound options and app-based tools, which can be combined with counseling and aural rehabilitation strategies.
ReSound Hearing Aids in Raleigh, NC

ReSound technology can be a strong fit for patients seeking natural listening, modern connectivity, and flexible options across hearing needs. At Raleigh Hearing and Tinnitus Center, we pair ReSound hearing aids with evidence-based fitting, including real ear verification and speech-in-noise testing, so your technology truly supports your life.
